You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the mbed-os codebase.
ARM Mbed OS is an embedded operating system for ARM Cortex-M microcontrollers. It includes a hardware abstraction layer (HAL), RTOS primitives, peripheral drivers (SPI, I2C, UART, CAN, USB), connectivity (BLE, Wi-Fi, cellular), storage, and security. C/C++ codebase.

TOOL USAGE — you MUST use these exact formats:

To run a shell command:
<bash>your_command_here</bash>

To view a file:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>

To view specific lines:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor> [start_line, end_line]

To edit a file (replace text):
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>exact text to replace</old_str>
<new_str>replacement text</new_str>

When finished, say: SUBMIT

IMPORTANT: Each response must contain EXACTLY ONE tool call. Do NOT put multiple tool calls in one response.

Working directory is /repo.
Think step by step. First understand the code, then investigate, then fix.

There is a missing NULL check after memory allocation that can fail downstream of function nrf_egu_int_enable in the targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x subsystem.
The function is located at targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x/TARGET_SDK_15_0/modules/nrfx/hal/nrf_egu.h:356.
Please investigate and fix this issue.

Let me first examine the function `nrf_egu_int_enable` in the specified file to understand the issue.

<bash>find /repo -name "nrf_egu.h" -type f</bash>

Exit code: 0
/repo/targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x/TARGET_SDK_15_0/modules/nrfx/hal/nrf_egu.h

Now let me examine the nrf_egu.h file, specifically around line 356 where the nrf_egu_int_enable function is located.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x/TARGET_SDK_15_0/modules/nrfx/hal/nrf_egu.h</str_replace_editor> [350, 370]

I can see the `nrf_egu_int_enable` function, but I need to understand the context better. The issue mentions a missing NULL check after memory allocation that can fail downstream of this function. Let me look at more of the file to understand how this function is used and what memory allocation might be involved.

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x/TARGET_SDK_15_0/modules/nrfx/hal/nrf_egu.h</str_replace_editor> [1, 50]

01	// NRFX_SWI_AVAILABLE_MASK - SWIs available for this module, i.e. present
   102	// in the hardware and neither reserved by external modules nor disabled
   103	// in <nrfx_config.h>.
   104	#define NRFX_SWI_PRESENT_MASK   ((1u << (SWI_COUNT)) - 1u)
   105	#define NRFX_SWI_AVAILABLE_MASK (NRFX_SWI_PRESENT_MASK &    \
   106	                                 ~(NRFX_SWI_RESERVED_MASK | \
   107	                                   NRFX_SWI_DISABLED_MASK))
   108	
   109	#if (NRFX_SWI_AVAILABLE_MASK == 0)
   110	#error "No available SWI instances. Check <nrfx_config.h> and NRFX_SWI_USED."
   111	#endif
   112	
   113	#define NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(idx)  ((NRFX_SWI_AVAILABLE_MASK >> (idx)) & 1u)
   114	
   115	#define NRFX_SWI_FIRST  (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(0) ? 0u : \
   116	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(1) ? 1u : \
   117	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(2) ? 2u : \
   118	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(3) ? 3u : \
   119	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(4) ? 4u : \
   120	                                                    5u)))))
   121	#define NRFX_SWI_LAST   (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(5) ? 5u : \
   122	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(4) ? 4u : \
   123	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(3) ? 3u : \
   124	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(2) ? 2u : \
   125	                        (NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(1) ? 1u : \
   126	                                                    0u)))))
   127	
   128	// NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT - number of EGU instances to be used by this module
   129	// (note - if EGU is not present, EGU_COUNT is not defined).
   130	#if NRFX_CHECK(NRFX_EGU_ENABLED)
   131	#define NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT  EGU_COUNT
   132	#else
   133	#define NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT  0
   134	#endif
   135	
   136	// These flags are needed only for SWIs that have no corresponding EGU unit
   137	// (in EGU such flags are available in hardware).
   138	#if (NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT < SWI_COUNT)
   139	static nrfx_swi_flags_t   m_swi_flags[SWI_COUNT - NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT];
   140	#endif
   141	static nrfx_swi_handler_t m_swi_handlers[SWI_COUNT];
   142	static uint8_t            m_swi_allocated_mask;
   143	
   144	
   145	static void swi_mark_allocated(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   146	{
   147	    m_swi_allocated_mask |= (1u << swi);
   148	}
   149	
   150	static void swi_mark_unallocated(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   151	{
   152	    m_swi_allocated_mask &= ~(1u << swi);
   153	}
   154	
   155	static bool swi_is_allocated(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   156	{
   157	    return (m_swi_allocated_mask & (1u << swi));
   158	}
   159	
   160	static bool swi_is_available(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   161	{
   162	    return NRFX_SWI_IS_AVAILABLE(swi);
   163	}
   164	
   165	static IRQn_Type swi_irq_number_get(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   166	{
   167	    return (IRQn_Type)((uint32_t)SWI0_IRQn + (uint32_t)swi);
   168	}
   169	
   170	static void swi_handler_setup(nrfx_swi_t         swi,
   171	                              nrfx_swi_handler_t event_handler,
   172	                              uint32_t           irq_priority)
   173	{
   174	    m_swi_handlers[swi] = event_handler;
   175	
   176	#if NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT
   177	    if (swi < NRFX_SWI_EGU_COUNT)
   178	    {
   179	        NRF_EGU_Type * p_egu = nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get(swi);
   180	        NRFX_ASSERT(p_egu != NULL);
   181	        nrf_egu_int_enable(p_egu, NRF_EGU_INT_ALL);
   182	
   183	        if (event_handler == NULL)
   184	        {
   185	            return;
   186	        }
   187	    }
   188	#endif
   189	
   190	    NRFX_ASSERT(event_handler != NULL);
   191	
   192	    NRFX_IRQ_PRIORITY_SET(swi_irq_number_get(swi), irq_priority);
   193	    NRFX_IRQ_ENABLE(swi_irq_number_get(swi));
   194	}
   195	
   196	nrfx_err_t nrfx_swi_alloc(nrfx_swi_t *       p_swi,
   197	                          nrfx_swi_handler_t event_handler,
   198	                          uint32_t           irq_priority)
   199	{
   200	    NRFX_ASSERT(p_swi != NULL);

Let me continue reading the nrfx_swi.c file to see the full implementation:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x/TARGET_SDK_15_0/modules/nrfx/drivers/src/nrfx_swi.c</str_replace_editor> [195, 250]

Now let me look at the `nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get` function that's being called in the swi_handler_setup function to understand the potential NULL pointer issue:

<bash>grep -r "nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get" /repo/targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x/ --include="*.c" --include="*.h"</bash>

Let me examine the nrfx_swi.h file to see the implementation of nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get:

    41	#ifndef NRFX_SWI_H__
    42	#define NRFX_SWI_H__
    43	
    44	#include <nrfx.h>
    45	
    46	#if NRFX_CHECK(NRFX_EGU_ENABLED)
    47	#include <hal/nrf_egu.h>
    48	#endif
    49	
    50	#ifdef __cplusplus
    51	extern "C" {
    52	#endif
    53	
    54	/**
    55	 * @defgroup nrfx_swi SWI driver
    56	 * @{
    57	 * @ingroup  nrf_swi_egu
    58	 *
    59	 * @brief    Driver for managing software interrupts (SWI).
    60	 */
    61	
    62	/** @brief SWI instance. */
    63	typedef uint8_t nrfx_swi_t;
    64	
    65	/**
    66	 * @brief SWI user flags.
    67	 *
    68	 * User flags are set during the SWI trigger and passed to the callback function as an argument.
    69	 */
    70	typedef uint16_t nrfx_swi_flags_t;
    71	
    72	/** @brief Unallocated instance value. */
    73	#define NRFX_SWI_UNALLOCATED  ((nrfx_swi_t)0xFFuL)
    74	
    75	/** @brief Default SWI priority. */
    76	#define NRFX_SWI_DEFAULT_PRIORITY  APP_IRQ_PRIORITY_LOWEST
    77	
    78	/**
    79	 * @brief SWI handler function.
    80	 *
    81	 * @param swi   SWI instance.
    82	 * @param flags User flags.
    83	 */
    84	typedef void (*nrfx_swi_handler_t)(nrfx_swi_t swi, nrfx_swi_flags_t flags);
    85	
    86	
    87	/**
    88	 * @brief Function for allocating the first unused SWI instance and setting a handler.
    89	 *
    90	 * @param[out] p_swi          Points to a place where the allocated SWI instance
    91	 *                            number is to be stored.
    92	 * @param[in]  event_handler  Event handler function.
    93	 *                            If NULL, no interrupt will be enabled.
    94	 *                            It can be NULL only if the EGU driver is enabled.
    95	 *                            For classic SWI, it must be a valid handler pointer.
    96	 * @param[in]  irq_priority   Interrupt priority.
    97	 *
    98	 * @retval NRFX_SUCCESS       If the SWI was successfully allocated.
    99	 * @retval NRFX_ERROR_NO_MEM  If there is no available SWI to be used.
   100	 */
   101	nrfx_err_t nrfx_swi_alloc(nrfx_swi_t *       p_swi,
   102	                          nrfx_swi_handler_t event_handler,
   103	                          uint32_t           irq_priority);
   104	
   105	/**
   106	 * @brief Function for freeing a previously allocated SWI.
   107	 *
   108	 * @param[in,out] p_swi  SWI instance to free. The value is changed to
   109	 *                       @ref NRFX_SWI_UNALLOCATED on success.
   110	 */
   111	void nrfx_swi_free(nrfx_swi_t * p_swi);
   112	
   113	/** @brief Function for freeing all allocated SWIs. */
   114	void nrfx_swi_all_free(void);
   115	
   116	/**
   117	 * @brief Function for triggering the SWI.
   118	 *
   119	 * @param[in] swi          SWI to trigger.
   120	 * @param[in] flag_number  Number of user flag to trigger.
   121	 */
   122	void nrfx_swi_trigger(nrfx_swi_t swi,
   123	                      uint8_t    flag_number);
   124	
   125	/**
   126	 * @brief Function for checking if the specified SWI is currently allocated.
   127	 *
   128	 * @param[in] swi  SWI instance.
   129	 *
   130	 * @retval true  If the SWI instance is allocated.
   131	 * @retval false Otherwise.
   132	 */
   133	bool nrfx_swi_is_allocated(nrfx_swi_t swi);
   134	
   135	#if NRFX_CHECK(NRFX_EGU_ENABLED) || defined(__NRFX_DOXYGEN__)
   136	
   137	/**
   138	 * @brief Function for returning the base address of the EGU peripheral
   139	 *        associated with the specified SWI instance.
   140	 *
   141	 * @param[in] swi  SWI instance.
   142	 *
   143	 * @returns EGU base address or NULL if the specified SWI instance number
   144	 *          is too high.
   145	 */
   146	__STATIC_INLINE NRF_EGU_Type * nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get(nrfx_swi_t swi)
   147	{
   148	#if (EGU_COUNT < SWI_COUNT)
   149	    if (swi >= EGU_COUNT)
   150	    {
   151	        return NULL;
   152	    }
   153	#endif
   154	    uint32_t offset = ((uint32_t)swi) * (NRF_EGU1_BASE - NRF_EGU0_BASE);
   155	    return (NRF_EGU_Type *)(NRF_EGU0_BASE + offset);
   156	}
   157	
   158	/**
   159	 * @brief Function for returning the EGU trigger task address.
   160	 *
   161	 * @param[in] swi      SWI instance.
   162	 * @param[in] channel  Number of the EGU channel.
   163	 *
   164	 * @returns Address of EGU trigger task.
   165	 */
   166	__STATIC_INLINE uint32_t nrfx_swi_task_trigger_address_get(nrfx_swi_t swi,
   167	                                                           uint8_t    channel)
   168	{
   169	    NRFX_ASSERT(nrfx_swi_is_allocated(swi));
   170	
   171	    NRF_EGU_Type * p_egu = nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get(swi);
   172	#if (EGU_COUNT < SWI_COUNT)
   173	    if (p_egu == NULL)
   174	    {
   175	        return 0;
   176	    }
   177	#endif
   178	
   179	    return (uint32_t)nrf_egu_task_trigger_address_get(p_egu, channel);
   180	}
   181	
   182	/**
   183	 * @brief Function for returning the EGU triggered event address.
   184	 *
   185	 * @param[in] swi      SWI instance.
   186	 * @param[in] channel  Number of the EGU channel.
   187	 *
   188	 * @returns Address of EGU triggered event.
   189	 */
   190	__STATIC_INLINE uint32_t nrfx_swi_event_triggered_address_get(nrfx_swi_t swi,
   191	                                                              uint8_t    channel)
   192	{
   193	    NRFX_ASSERT(nrfx_swi_is_allocated(swi));
   194	
   195	    NRF_EGU_Type * p_egu = nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get(swi);
   196	#if (EGU_COUNT < SWI_COUNT)
   197	    if (p_egu == NULL)
   198	    {
   199	        return 0;
   200	    }

Now I can see the issue! Looking at the code, I can see that in the `swi_handler_setup` function in nrfx_swi.c, there's a call to `nrfx_swi_egu_instance_get(swi)` which can return NULL, and this NULL value is then passed to `nrf_egu_int_enable` without a NULL check.

Let me examine the specific problematic code in the swi_handler_setup function:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/targets/TARGET_NORDIC/TARGET_NRF5x/TARGET_SDK_15_0/modules/nrfx/drivers/src/nrfx_swi.c</str_replace_editor> [170, 195]

I can see the issue now. In the `swi_handler_setup` function, there's already an assertion `NRFX_ASSERT(p_egu != NULL);` that checks if the EGU instance is not NULL, but there's no actual runtime check. When `nrf_egu_int_enable` is called with a potentially NULL pointer, it will cause undefined behavior.
